criteria.name: `tests/gpu_orchestrator/runpod/test_startup_script.py` passes, including the existing `test_rendered_startup_script_bootstraps_uv_and_runs_locked_sync` (proving the literal `uv sync --locked --python 3.10 --extra cuda124` invocation is preserved) and a new test asserting the sentinel-skip contract.
The full `pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator` suite passes — no collateral regression.
The rendered startup script parses cleanly under `bash -n` (no shell syntax errors introduced).

The sentinel hash input pipeline references all five required inputs in the rendered script: `uv.lock` contents (via `sha256sum uv.lock`), `pyproject.toml` contents (via `sha256sum pyproject.toml`), `python: 3.10`, `extras: cuda124`, and `"$UV_BIN" --version` output.
`pyproject.toml` is included in the sentinel hash so that any edit to it forces the sync path to run, preserving `uv sync --locked` as the backstop against pyproject/lock drift (FLAG-001).
The sentinel file path in the rendered script is `.venv/.sync-inputs` (under `.venv/` so deleting `.venv` self-heals).

The sentinel is written only *after* the `"$UV_BIN" sync --locked …` line in the rendered script, and any prior sentinel is `rm -f`'d *before* the sync runs (so an interrupted sync leaves no stale marker; `set -e` ensures the post-sync write is unreachable on failure).
`update_worker_phase "deps_verified"` appears exactly once and sits *after* the closing `fi` of the new if/else — so it fires on both skip and sync paths.
On first-time provisioning (no `.venv`, no sentinel), the rendered logic falls through to the full sync path — verifiable by inspecting the `if [ -d .venv ] && [ -f "$SYNC_SENTINEL" ]` guard.
The submodule reconcile block at lines 270–293 of `worker_startup.template.sh` is unchanged in the diff.
The skip-path log line is human-readable and includes the computed hash and uv version (so log readers can diagnose unexpected skips/non-skips at a glance).

On a real warm worker boot with unchanged `uv.lock` and `pyproject.toml`, the worker startup log shows the `⏭️  Skipping uv sync` line and the boot is materially faster (≈90 s saved); on a cold boot or post-lockfile/pyproject-edit boot it shows the `✅ uv sync complete; …refreshed` line.

summary: Light robustness: review skipped; stub written for artifact parity.
